tagline: From a bachelor's degree to Principal Aerospace Engineer — two promotions, ten years.

start.title: Aerospace Engineer
start.note: You are here — a bachelor's degree, ~0 years of experience. You run assigned analyses and design components under a senior engineer's direction.

rungs.title: Senior Aerospace Engineer
rungs.scope: Own an aircraft or spacecraft subsystem end-to-end — design, analysis, test, and qualification — instead of assigned tasks.
rungs.experience: ~5–8 years, typically with a PE license. You own analyses and sign off on your components.
rungs.evaluated: Evidence you own components end-to-end: designs you took through the review gates, analyses you sign off, a PE license, and peers who trust your engineering judgment.

rungs.title: Lead / Principal Aerospace Engineer
rungs.scope: Set technical direction across a program — the system architecture and integration several teams build against — on the chief-engineer track.
rungs.experience: ~10–15+ years. A promotion gated on system-level ownership and program delivery, not tenure.
rungs.evaluated: Sustained program-level impact: a system architecture teams build to, programs delivered through PDR/CDR/qualification, and technical authority recognised across the org.
